Title: Decaprenyl diphosphate synthases in Monolepta hieroglyphica: DPPS-mediated CoQ biosynthesis impacts juvenile hormone and gut microbiota

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Monolepta hieroglyphica; Escherichia coli; Drosophila melanogaster
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Monolepta hieroglyphica; Escherichia coli; Drosophila melanogaster
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: cis/prenyltransferase-related decaprenyl diphosphate synthase; contains six conserved motifs and three Asp-rich DDxxD motifs
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Ubiquinone (coenzyme Q) and terpenoid backbone/isoprenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: CoQ side-chain prenyl donor synthesis; terpenoid backbone branch connected to juvenile hormone precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: (E)-GPP; (E)-FPP; (E)-GGPP in vitro; required with MhieDPPS2 for CoQ9 and CoQ10 production in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers isopentenyl units to allylic prenyl diphosphates; catalyzes formation of (E)-GPP and (E)-FPP from IPP + DMAPP and forms (E)-FPP and (E)-GGPP from IPP + (E)-GPP; together with MhieDPPS2 enables CoQ9/CoQ10 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes CoQ9 and CoQ10 biosynthesis; knockdown lowers CoQ9/CoQ10 and increases juvenile hormone III; influences survival and developmental/metabolic phenotypes
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies prenyl side-chain precursors and is required for target-product formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Electron transport, antioxidant defense, metabolism, growth and lifespan regulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Adult beetle body; detected in adult Monolepta hieroglyphica
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in maize-fed insects than soybean-fed insects; high in female abdomen; elevated in female gonad; higher in females than males
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In M. hieroglyphica, significantly upregulated in maize-fed beetles; in transgenic Drosophila overexpressed from UAS construct
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; recombinant enzyme assay with GC-MS; Michaelis-Menten kinetics; molecular docking; co-expression in E. coli with LC-MS detection of CoQ; RNAi knockdown with ELISA metabolite measurement; transgenic overexpression in Drosophila; transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point in CoQ biosynthesis in this study, but not definitively established as sole rate-limiting enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Host plant diet (maize versus soybean); dsRNA RNAi; ubiquinol supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown reduced survival; overexpression in Drosophila extended developmental period and altered metabolic networks/gut microbiota
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UAS promoter in Drosophila transgenic model (inducible GAL4/UAS system)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome sequencing (Illumina NovaSeq 6000); gut microbiome sequencing (Illumina MiSeq); accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential molecular target for environmentally friendly pest management by disrupting CoQ biosynthesis and associated physiology
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MhieDPPS1 alone showed broad prenyltransferase activity in vitro, but CoQ9/CoQ10 production in vivo required co-expression with MhieDPPS2; study also links DPPS function to inverse changes in juvenile hormone III
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MhieDPPS1 expression is higher in maize-fed beetles where CoQ9/CoQ10 are elevated and JHIII is reduced. 2. Recombinant MhieDPPS1 catalyzes versatile prenyl transfer, producing (E)-GPP, (E)-FPP and (E)-GGPP from multiple substrates in vitro. 3. Co-expression with MhieDPPS2 in E. coli is necessary for CoQ9 and CoQ10 production. 4. RNAi knockdown decreases CoQ9/CoQ10, increases JHIII, and lowers survival, demonstrating causal involvement in CoQ metabolism and physiology.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Monolepta hieroglyphica; Escherichia coli; Drosophila melanogaster
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Monolepta hieroglyphica; Escherichia coli; Drosophila melanogaster
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Required with MhieDPPS1 for CoQ9 and CoQ10 production in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions jointly with MhieDPPS1 in decaprenyl side-chain formation necessary for CoQ9 and CoQ10 biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports CoQ9 and CoQ10 biosynthesis; knockdown contributes to reduced CoQ levels, elevated juvenile hormone III, and reduced survival
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for direct formation of target-product side chain together with MhieDPPS1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Electron transport, antioxidant defense, metabolism, growth and lifespan regulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Adult beetle body; detected in adult Monolepta hieroglyphica
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: No significant diet-dependent difference by RT-qPCR; highly expressed in female abdomen and intestine; higher in females than males
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in transgenic Drosophila together with MhieDPPS1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; co-expression in E. coli with LC-MS detection of CoQ; RNAi knockdown with ELISA metabolite measurement; tissue expression analysis; transgenic overexpression in Drosophila; transcriptome analysis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Required component of the CoQ biosynthetic module; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Host plant diet; dsRNA RNAi; ubiquinol supplementation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockdown reduced survival; overexpression with MhieDPPS1 in Drosophila extended developmental period and altered microbiome
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: UAS promoter in Drosophila transgenic model (inducible GAL4/UAS system)
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome sequencing (Illumina NovaSeq 6000); gut microbiome sequencing (Illumina MiSeq); accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential pest-control target when jointly disrupted with MhieDPPS1 to impair CoQ biosynthesis and insect fitness
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No CoQ production detected when MhieDPPS2 was expressed alone in E. coli; functional interdependence with MhieDPPS1 is central conclusion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MhieDPPS2 is a second DPPS subunit cloned from M. hieroglyphica and groups with insect DPPS2 proteins. 2. Expression alone in E. coli does not produce CoQ9/CoQ10, but co-expression with MhieDPPS1 enables both products. 3. RNAi of MhieDPPS2 contributes to reduced CoQ and increased JHIII, supporting an essential in vivo role. 4. Tissue expression is especially high in female intestine, indicating functional specialization.
